TF-E2.Summary: MVP 2: Collaboration — Notifications, Attachments, Search, Timeline
TF-E2.Description:
Extend TaskFlow AI with collaboration features: in-app notifications, validated file attachments with signed URLs, full-text search and filters, activity timeline, transactional email, and Redis caching for sessions and rate limits.

SCOPE:
- In-app notification system (task assigned, comment added, due date reminder)
- File attachments on tasks with MIME validation and signed URL access
- Search across tasks, projects, comments with filters (status, priority, assignee, date range)
- Activity timeline per project and task (audit-derived feed)
- Email notifications via transactional provider (Resend/SendGrid)
- Redis integration for session cache and API rate limiting
- Frontend components for notifications bell, search bar, attachment upload, timeline

EFFORT: L (50 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Full Stack
DEPENDENCIES: TF-E1 complete — auth, CRUD, RBAC operational

EPIC LINK: epic/taskflow-implementation
FEATURE BRANCH: epic/TF-E2-collaboration

REFERENCE DOCS:
- docs/TaskFlowAI_Project_Proposal.md (§ MVP 2)
- docs/SECURITY.md (file validation, SSRF allowlists)

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- User receives in-app notification when assigned a task
- User uploads attachment <10MB, downloads via time-limited signed URL
- Search returns relevant tasks within 200ms P95
- Activity timeline shows last 50 events per project
- Email sent on task assignment (configurable opt-out)
- Redis rate limiting blocks >100 req/min per user with 429

Implement notification model, service, API, and real-time polling UI for workspace events.

USE CASES:
- User assigned to task receives notification 'You were assigned to Fix login bug'
- User clicks notification bell and sees unread count badge
- User marks notification as read; badge count decrements
- User marks all as read

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create Notification model: id, user_id, workspace_id, type, title, body, resource_type, resource_id, read_at, created_at
- Alembic migration 002_notifications
- Create backend/services/notification_service.py: create_notification, list_unread, mark_read, mark_all_read
- Create backend/api/v1/notifications.py: GET /notifications, PATCH /notifications/{id}/read, POST /notifications/read-all
- Emit notifications from task_service on assign, comment_service on new comment
- Create frontend/components/NotificationBell.tsx with polling every 30s (TanStack Query refetchInterval)

EFFORT: M (6 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend, Frontend
DEPENDENCIES: TF-008

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Assign task creates notification for assignee
- List returns only current user's notifications
- mark_read sets read_at timestamp
- Unread count accurate in API response

EDGE CASES:
- Self-assignment: optional notification (configurable, default skip)
- Notification for deleted task: body shows '[deleted]' without 500
- >100 unread: paginate with limit=50 default
- Cross-workspace notification access: 404

TF-E2.Summary: File Attachments with Validation and Signed URLs
TF-E2.Description:
Implement secure file upload for task attachments with MIME validation, size limits, and signed download URLs.

USE CASES:
- User attaches PDF screenshot to bug report task
- User downloads attachment via signed URL valid for 15 minutes
- User cannot upload executable (.exe) or oversized file
- Manager views all attachments on task detail page

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create Attachment model: id, task_id, filename, mime_type, size_bytes, storage_key, uploaded_by, workspace_id
- Alembic migration 003_attachments
- Storage: Supabase Storage or S3-compatible with workspace-prefixed keys
- Create backend/services/attachment_service.py: validate_mime (allowlist: image/*, application/pdf, text/plain), max 10MB
- POST /api/v1/tasks/{id}/attachments (multipart), GET /api/v1/attachments/{id}/download (signed URL)
- SSRF defense: no user-supplied URLs in storage layer
- Create frontend/components/AttachmentUpload.tsx with drag-drop and progress

EFFORT: L (8 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend, Frontend, Security
DEPENDENCIES: TF-008

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Upload PNG succeeds, returns attachment metadata
- Upload .exe rejected with 415
- Upload >10MB rejected with 413
- Signed URL expires after TTL
- Download without valid signature returns 403

EDGE CASES:
- Upload to task in another workspace: 404
- Duplicate filename: allow with unique storage_key
- Storage service unavailable: 503 with retry-after
- Virus scan hook: stub interface for future ClamAV integration

Implement full-text search and structured filters across tasks, projects, and comments.

USE CASES:
- User searches 'login timeout' and sees matching tasks and comments
- User filters tasks by status=IN_PROGRESS and priority=HIGH
- User filters by assignee and due date range
- Manager searches within single project scope

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/api/v1/search.py: GET /api/v1/search?q=&type=tasks|projects|comments&filters...
- PostgreSQL: tsvector on task.title, task.description, comment.body with GIN index
- Filters: project_id, status, priority, assignee_id, due_before, due_after
- Create backend/services/search_service.py with workspace_id mandatory scope
- Pagination: limit max 50, cursor-based for large result sets
- Create frontend/components/SearchBar.tsx with debounced input and filter chips

EFFORT: M (6 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend, Frontend
DEPENDENCIES: TF-008, TF-009

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Search 'login' returns tasks with login in title or description
- Filter status=TODO excludes IN_PROGRESS tasks
- Cross-workspace results never returned
- Empty query with filters returns filtered list
- P95 latency <200ms on 1000 task dataset (benchmark test)

EDGE CASES:
- SQL injection in q param: parameterized query only
- Special chars in search: escape for tsquery
- No results: return empty array with total=0
- Search across archived tasks: exclude by default, ?include_archived=true for admin

TF-E2.Summary: Activity Timeline per Project and Task
TF-E2.Description:
Build activity feed derived from audit logs and entity events for project and task views.

USE CASES:
- User views project page and sees timeline: 'Alice created task X', 'Bob commented on Y'
- User views task detail sidebar with chronological activity
- Timeline shows actor name, action, timestamp, relative time ('2 hours ago')

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/api/v1/activity.py: GET /projects/{id}/activity, GET /tasks/{id}/activity
- Create backend/services/activity_service.py: aggregate from audit_log + comment/task events
- ActivityEvent schema: id, actor, action, resource, summary, created_at
- Limit 50 events per request, cursor pagination for history
- Create frontend/components/ActivityTimeline.tsx with icons per action type
- Extend audit_service to capture comment.created, task.status_changed with metadata

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend, Frontend
DEPENDENCIES: TF-011, TF-008

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Task create appears in project activity within same request cycle
- Comment add appears in task activity
- Timeline ordered descending by created_at
- Non-member cannot view project activity (403)

EDGE CASES:
- Deleted user actor: show 'Former member'
- Bulk task import: collapse into single activity event
- Activity for soft-deleted task: show with archived badge
- Empty timeline: friendly empty state message

Send transactional emails for task assignment and due date reminders with user opt-out preference.

USE CASES:
- User assigned to task receives email with task title and link
- User receives due date reminder 24 hours before deadline
- User disables email notifications in preferences; no emails sent
- Email contains no sensitive data beyond task title and link

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Add resend or sendgrid SDK to pyproject.toml
- Create backend/services/email_service.py: send_task_assigned, send_due_reminder
- Create UserPreference model field: email_notifications_enabled (default true)
- HTML email templates in backend/templates/email/ (minimal, no tracking pixels)
- Background job: APScheduler or FastAPI BackgroundTasks for due date scan (daily cron)
- Settings: EMAIL_API_KEY, EMAIL_FROM_ADDRESS

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: TF-013

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Assign task triggers email when preference enabled
- Preference disabled: no email sent, in-app notification still created
- Email template renders task title and deep link URL
- Mock email provider in tests; assert call count

EDGE CASES:
- Invalid email address on user: skip send, log warning
- Email provider rate limit: retry with tenacity 3x
- Due reminder for completed task: skip
- Unsubscribe link in email footer (future: one-click opt-out token)

Integrate Redis for session cache, API rate limiting, and foundation for MVP 3 working memory.

USE CASES:
- Repeated GET /api/v1/projects served from cache within TTL (optional)
- User exceeding 100 requests/minute receives 429 Too Many Requests
- Login endpoint limited to 10 attempts/minute per IP
- Redis connection pooled for async FastAPI

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Add redis[hiredis] to pyproject.toml
- Create backend/cache/redis_client.py: async Redis connection pool from REDIS_URL
- Create backend/middleware/rate_limit.py: sliding window per user_id and per IP
- Rate limits: global 100/min user, auth 10/min IP, search 30/min user
- Add redis service to infrastructure/docker-compose.yml
- Headers: X-RateLimit-Remaining, Retry-After on 429
- Metrics: rate_limit_exceeded_total counter

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend, Infrastructure
DEPENDENCIES: TF-012

TESTING CRITERIA:
- 101st request in minute returns 429
- Rate limit resets after window
- Redis down: fail open with warning log (not 500 on all requests)
- rate_limit_exceeded_total increments

EDGE CASES:
- Unauthenticated requests: rate limit by IP only
- Admin role: optional higher limit (200/min)
- Redis connection timeout: 2s max, degrade gracefully
- Health check does not count toward rate limit

Integrate notification bell, search, attachments, and activity timeline into main app layout.

USE CASES:
- User navigates app with notification bell always visible in header
- User searches from global search bar in navbar
- Task detail page shows attachments section and activity sidebar
- All collaboration features respect auth and workspace scope

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Update frontend/app/layout.tsx: header with NotificationBell, SearchBar
- Update task detail page: AttachmentUpload list, ActivityTimeline sidebar
- User preferences page: email notification toggle
- TanStack Query cache invalidation on notification read, attachment upload
- Vitest tests for NotificationBell, SearchBar, ActivityTimeline

EFFORT: M (6 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Frontend
DEPENDENCIES: TF-013, TF-014, TF-015, TF-016, TF-010

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Vitest coverage >75% on new components
- Search debounce 300ms prevents excessive API calls
- Attachment list refreshes after upload without page reload
- WCAG: notification bell has aria-label

EDGE CASES:
- Slow network: skeleton loaders on timeline and attachments
- Search with no results: empty state with clear filters CTA
- Mobile: notification dropdown scrollable, max-height 80vh

End-to-end integration tests for collaboration features and update PLAN.md.

USE CASES:
- CI runs full collaboration flow test on every PR
- docs/PLAN.md reflects TF-E2 completion status
- docs/learning/mvp2-collaboration.md captures patterns used

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/tests/integration/test_collaboration_flow.py: assign → notify → comment → activity → search
- Create backend/tests/integration/test_attachments.py: upload, download, reject invalid mime
- Create backend/tests/integration/test_rate_limit.py: 429 after threshold
- Update docs/PLAN.md with TF-013 through TF-020 status
- Write docs/learning/mvp2-collaboration.md
- Update docs/tasks/todo.md epic TF-E2 section

EFFORT: S (4 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Testing, Documentation
DEPENDENCIES: TF-013, TF-014, TF-015, TF-016, TF-017, TF-018, TF-019

TESTING CRITERIA:
- All integration tests pass in CI
- Backend coverage remains >80%
- PLAN.md lists TF-E2 tasks with checkboxes

EDGE CASES:
- Integration tests use testcontainers Redis or fakeredis
- Email tests use mock, never send real email in CI
- File upload tests use in-memory storage driver
